topicData.topic: Why construction supply firms are losing AI adoption races to telecoms operators — and it's because their workforce isn't sitting at desks
topicData.angle: You can't pilot an AI chatbot in a construction supply firm the same way you pilot it in a telecoms call centre. And most organisations don't realise they're using the wrong playbook until 6 months in.

postText:
Most AI adoption playbooks were written for people sitting at desks.

That's not a small assumption. It's the whole thing.

In a telecoms operator, the workflow already lives on a screen. The agent is there, the interface is there, you drop in the tool and it fits. I watched this work at scale. It genuinely does.

In a construction supply firm, your people are in a yard, on a lorry, on a site. They're not waiting to type into a chatbot. The interface assumption is wrong from day one, and most programmes don't discover that until month six when adoption numbers are embarrassing.

That's not an AI problem. It's a where-are-your-people-actually-standing problem.

And I don't think there's one clean answer here. Voice interfaces help. So does embedding the tool into whatever device is already in their hand. But I've seen both struggle depending on the operation.

If you're running AI adoption in a distribution or construction supply business right now, what's actually working on the ground?

#TechLeadership
